description: This figure presents ex-situ X-ray diffraction (XRD) analysis revealing the phase evolution of mechanically alloyed Fe40Mn19Ni15Al15Si10C1 high entropy steel powder upon annealing at various temperatures. Panel (a) displays the full XRD patterns from room temperature (R.T.) through 400°C, 500°C, 600°C to 800°C, identifying four distinct phases: BCC (body-centered cubic, ♥), B2-type ordered phase (◆), γ-brass type phase (♣), and Fe5Si3-type silicide (●). Panel (b) provides an enlarged view of the 2θ range 42°–46°, highlighting the complex peak splitting and shifting near the primary (110) reflection. Panels (c)–(e) present deconvoluted peak fittings for samples annealed at 400°C, 500°C, and 600°C respectively, quantitatively resolving the overlapping contributions from BCC, B2-type, and γ-brass type phases, while panel (f) shows the enlarged 40°–46° region for 800°C, clearly resolving the Fe5Si3-type phase peaks alongside BCC and B2-type reflections. The data demonstrates that the 35-hour milled powder remains thermally stable up to 500°C, with the exothermic event at ~420°C corresponding to ordering/texturing of the γ-brass type phase and the event at ~520°C marking the onset of Fe5Si3-type intermetallic formation, which becomes clearly detectable at 600°C and persists at 800°C where the B2-type phase fraction additionally increases.
